rationale: The only resume change swapped lesser-known employers (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) for famous brands (Stripe, Shopify, Datadog), yet both evaluations land on the identical 3/10 score and rest on the same disqualifying rationale: far short of the required 8 years of C++ (only ~7 months) and a background skewed toward full-stack web/DLang rather than low-level cloud compute. The prestigious employer names earned the candidate no extra credit and triggered no new concerns. The differences are which already-present accomplishments get spotlighted (GISCollective founding vs. Dunnhumby throughput) — ordinary run-to-run variation, not reasoning that keys off the brand swap.
